subject: Three small wild boar piglets standing alert in a forest clearing, their backs to the viewer as they sense an unseen predator approaching from the woods
description: A trio of striped boar piglets stand in a sunlit forest clearing, their small bodies tense and alert. Their tan-and-brown striped coats — the characteristic camouflage of young wild boars — blend into the forest floor of dead leaves and undergrowth. The piglets face away from the viewer, looking into the deeper woods as though they have just heard or scented something dangerous. Tall dark tree trunks rise around the clearing, and warm green light filters through a thick canopy, giving the scene a humid, enclosed woodland feel. The composition is quiet and tense — these are the quarry of the card title, prey that does not yet know it is doomed.
foreground: Three striped boar piglets standing on a forest floor of dead leaves and roots
background: Dense forest with tall dark tree trunks and green dappled light filtering through the canopy
emotion: alert, wary, frozen mid-step — prey that has just sensed danger
